prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: I think one under-discussed writer is David Foster Wallace. While his magnum opus, "Infinite Jest," is widely read and discussed, some of his other works, such as "The Pale King" and his essays and short stories, deserve more attention for their insightful commentary on contemporary society and his unique writing style. Many people overlook the depth and complexity of his ideas, focusing only on his intricate and challenging writing style. Wallace's works require and reward multiple readings and deep engagement from the reader. I think everyone should read more of David Foster Wallace's works beyond just "Infinite Jest." His insights into American society, culture, and the human psyche are invaluable and demonstrate why he is one of the most important writers of the late 20th and early 21st century.
